reviews.review_description: The art style in this game is amazing! The gameplay is great as well! If it’s catches your eye then trust your gut and buy it! You won’t be disappointed.
Great enemies and bosses, fantastic use of sound and light, easy to understand weapons and VR mechanics.  This was absolutely one of the best experiences I have had in VR.

reviews.review_title: We need more games like this
reviews.review_description: All five stars are deserved here. This is my favorite VR horror title. We need Drifter to put more games like this on the quest store. The story, the mechanics, and the weapon play are all fantastic in this title. This game is worth anyone’s time because it is an absolute banger. And when they say hard mode is super hard, they mean it; I spent the better part of 5 hours beating it. Probably why only 89 other people (as of now) have the achievement for beating hard mode. Over all, best vr horror game I’ve played.

My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.

It's a great survival horror with satisfying shooting and melee combat. Very tense at times so be prepared.. Good graphics and sound effects. Not much replayability and sometimes annoying difficulty spikes which made me change its difficulty level. Recommended buy on sale.
This game is a mash up of Evil Dead, Resident Evil, and The Evil Within.
This game with endless will go off the rail. The game is amazing, only held back by replayability
Good graphics, good story, good jumpscares. It’s great

The mechanics are simple, easy to learn or at least remember if you've played any shooter on these headsets, in fact it's even easier simce you dont need to manually load the gun, just open the chamber and bullets fill the slots. The atmosphere is oppressive, just like how you'd want a horror game to feel and I love the comic style but I don't think it meshes with a horror theme because the enemies, in my opinion, look kind of goofy with this style.

I'm having issues with the melee that I'm not sure are intended features, bugs, or my old headset: Melee weapons like the hatchet get stuck in enemies while I'm swinging so I have to either finish them with the pistol (what I wanted to avoid using by using the axe) or risk grabbing it out of them while they're still attacking. Nothing game-breaking, just curious if that's intentional.

This game has an amazing atmosphere and simplistic mechanics which makes it one of the few titles I’ve played start to finish. The art and imagery are top notch and I truly loved the entire experience! Buy this game!!

This is easily one of the best quest games,it's original, graphics and story are amazing,and the mechanics are so good.Ive been playing computer games since I was little and this one stood out.well done developers pure masterpiece of a horror game in vr.

Haven't played this in a while and I just noticed I never wrote a review for it.
If you like a mature horror, based shooter story, this is a solid one. 
I got it on my quest 2 soon after I got it and its a by far 1 of those games that even if you haven't played for a year and forgot what's it al about you will always remember that this is a top notch horror shooter.

They don’t come much better than this. It’s kind of a shooter so you start to forget how scary it is and then it reminds you. The game is a master class in VR graphics design/presentation. I can’t think of any other game to compare this to, except for superfluous comparisons like RE4. Lies Beneath is a top quality experience that belongs in your library.
Great artstyle, great tension, and great combat. Very under-rated. Especially worth a purchase on a sale.

reviews.review_title: Great art style and story telling
reviews.review_description:
I was sat on the fence with buying this game for a while but thought I'd give it a go when it was on offer. This is partly due to the cell shaded art style, lack of any Q3 specific update and 2020 release date. However, upon playing the game, I was immediately drawn in. I think the cell shaded art works brilliantly and looks very sharp on the Q3. I honestly don't think it needs a Q3 update and I'm not sure what you'd enhance anyway. The art style also lends perfectly to the graphic novel approach to the storytelling. I was eager to see what each new chapter would bring.

I played through the entire game on "normal" difficulty level and I found it quite challenging on a few occasions when I'd die repeatedly. The enemies are menacing, look great and offer a decent amount of variety. The game doesn't allow you to stockpile a tremendous amount of ammo either. For instance, you can only ever carry 6 shell shotgun shells at a time. That's it. So you have to use it sparingly, but it makes you use a variety of weapons and manage your resources carefully.

I can tell the devs put a lot of love and thought into this game with the little touches and ideas implemented. For example, the way you display damage. There is no health bar. Instead, you know by looking at how bloody your hands are. Even the menu/title screen is interesting and keeps you second-guessing as it evolves as you progress through the game. *Hint: Look around and behind you and you may notice a few things at key points in the game 😉 *

Bravo devs.👏

I like the game, graphics. It would be better if it was narrated rather than reading everything, imo. I also have a glitch or something... When I die, it gets stuck in the screen telling me that, I then have to press oculus button as if I'm quitting, hit resume to get to the contine game screen only to see it won't let me. So I have to completely quit game, restart it to continue play from my last saved spot.  Very annoying. Makes me just want to play till I die once, then move on to another game. If it's fixable, I'll change my rating.

Probably the best game on the quest! If you play it on a quest 3, there are better games out there but this is an excelent horror action game if you can look past the small bugs (like not being able to pick up a dropped gun and generally the grabbing mechanic not working exactly as expected).    
The bad grabbing does lead to some frustrating moments but overall it is a stellar experience with great creppy moments, good boss fights and a decent story.

Lies Beneath has a distinct visual style that merges Lovecraftian imagery and comic book visuals, which is a very cool way to tell this story. The key to succeeding in Lies Beneath is your lighter, which is a multi-purpose tool that provides lore, exposes enemy weak points, and even shows you the direction you should be going. The combat can get a tad repetitive, and the difficulty can range from mind-numbing let easy to frustratingly difficult, but overall, this game offers a memorably chilling experience!

reviews.review_title: A Fine Horror Flight That's Flawed
reviews.review_description: It's fine. It's buggy, has lousy hit detection and the story is just meh. It achieves horror by screeching in your ears for the entirety of its four to six hour play time. Cheap jump scares mix up the monotony from time to time. But still, some decent stylistic choices and some creepy artistry keep its head above barely passable. Get it on sale and you likely won't be disappointed.

This game is intense,from the creepy atmosphere to the wicked creatures that are hot on your tail,it is not for the faint hearted. Having nothing more than a lighter to light your path in the darkness and a very limited supply of ammo,it will keep you on the edge of your seat. I love the idea of the amount of blood on your hands being used to gauge your health instead of an immersion breaking health bar. It can be hard at times because sometimes you will end up surrounded by an overwhelming amount of enemies. You cannot solely depend on your guns either,you better keep a melee weapon on you at all times. From the cool story to the comic book style graphics,this game is a winner. The fact this game was developed for the Quest 1,hasn’t been graphically updated for the Quest 2 and Quest 3 is quite impressive as all these years later,I remains an excellent game .. I highly recommend it
Got almost to the end, game won't load past the optimizing graphics startup screen even with rebooting the device. Very unfortunate. Nothing online about a fix either.

reviews.review_title: Quite difficult even on easy mode
reviews.review_description: Very good looking game with fantastic climate and interesting story. The sound is awesome making it a really breathtaking experience, but it's difficult and sometimes very scary. When you try to escape from the enemy and after awhile you slow down it feels like in the horror dream when you running away but you can't increase the distance. I wish the number of enemies to be more balanced for the casual player, hence 4 stars. It's not for everyone but it's good.

Would have given it 4 stars if it wasn’t for the limited ammo, lack of inventory, and idiotic save points. Need melee weapon? Better carry it in your hand and don’t lose it because you will need all of your storage for guns as there is not enough ammo for you to just get by with just one pistol. Gave up on the story and just plowed through the game just to finish it. Last boss fight was the peak of frustration, they take the already bad controls and invert them for the entire boss fight. Did the developers even play the game?     ￼
This game looks incredible of the 3S. The visuals and gameplay are excellent. It's terrifyingly gripping and really hard to put down. I can't fault it.

What i thought was the end of the game, wasn't even halfway through, I was so content I wouldn't have been surprised/disappointed if it had been. The story is fascinating, and confusing, by the time you reach the end it makes more sense but blimey is it a ride
The game is obviously made in the early days of VR. It has some interesting and fun parts. But as a whole, there are too many mechanics that just does does not work well.
This game is a huge waste of money beat the game on the hardest difficulty in a hour and this game has no replay value

I think that I understand the story, but only because I searched through the pile of story bits, that the game haphazardly dumps on the floor in front of you; Most of the bits don't actually connect to anything, and didn't have real purpose beyond stringing you along until the end. 

I actually appreciate the work the developers put into it; Making VR games has got to be way harder than making traditional games, and the creators clearly put work into it... But it's just as clear that this isn't a refined product.

Lies Beneath is a well put together proof of concept, but nothing more than that.
I played this game when it first came out. I have yet to find a horror game with this kind of experience.  It's definitely one of my all-time favorites. Highly recommend

I absolutely love It’s comic book, art style and Cthulhu horror themed world.
Scary and eerie 
Decent range in Meele combat .
Definitely worth paying for
This game is true vr horror jumpscares out of nowhere amazing art style and like a true horror game a very confusing story overall a masterpiece
https://www.oculus .com/appreferrals/Julnx/1706349256136062/?utm_source=3

Possibly the best single player game in the store. A great example that you can make a game for Quest with a good story, acceptable length and good gameplay and mechanics. 
The comic book look and feel is very successful. It saves resources on the standalone headset without losing immersion. It is important to mention the sound design of this game, which I find exquisite. It is mandatory to play it with headphones for a full experience.
Get ready to get into this nightmarish experience that will keep you on edge all the time and that at various times will put your heart in a fist. I love how several of the game's situations have been maliciously designed :D

To the people at Drifter: please, we want more games like this. Have you thought about what an Evil Dead game in this style would be like? It could be even crazier. It would be awesome!

However, there's a problem: it doesn't use cloud save and also, there's no way to manually save the game. So if for some reason you  need to temporarily uninstall the game, you lose all your progress. To the developers: please fix this.

This game is unplayable if you are bedridden.  I'm done with this.  I recommend anyone else who is frustrated by this or any other game like this to file a report with the ADA.  It's 2022 this is RIDICULOUS!  I get so little money from disability, despite working and paying taxes for decades, and these people can't make a simple patch to their game.  #DISGUSTED
Comic book horror game, it's better than resident  evil 4 🥰
I am a kid and I was playing this game and I almost had a heart attack

Great game. It definitely has its moments when it can scare the crap out of you. However, i feel like it has more action/shooter than horror. Still scary, but your not completely helpless, and that what i was looking for.

It’s really fun and has great graphics and runs really smooth. The turns are a little choppy which is unfortunate but not a big deal. I don’t usually get motion sick, but this game killed me. I had a stomach ache and headache for around 9 hours after. So it’s definitely not a game you can play straight through.
This was really, really bad.

I bought the game because it said it was a "shooter "game  I watched the video and thought for sure I was buying a good game because the price was $30 it was. After buying the game I realized I had been ripped off this game sucks do not buy this game is this game

I am absolutely blown away with how good this game is. The art style is super cool and the creepy atmosphere is ridiculously immersive. Gameplay is really fun and the storyline is well developed. You will be fighting for your live at pretty much every turn. There were challenging portions of the game that took me quite a few attempts to complete despite the fact that I like to think I'm pretty good at FPS games. Resources are plentiful, but due to the amount of enemies, you'll still have to be smart about how you use them. This game is by far one of the BEST GAMES I have played on the Quest 2. The campaign is lengthy without bordering on monotonous and there are plenty of new monsters and horrifying visuals around every corner to keep you on your toes. Boss battles are unique, challenging and straight up terrifying at times. The only complaint I have is that it is somewhat difficult to pick up items off the ground. You have to pretty much kneel all the way down to pick it up, but it's not so bad that it ruins the game or anything. Definitely worth your time and money and the replay value, for me personally,  is very high. Phenomenal game. Hope to see more from this developer in the future.

The only thing good about this game is the atmosphere and how it looks. The gameplay makes me want to cry.
Not only are the monsters braindead meatshields that just walk towards you they also deal a lot of damage. What do you do when you take damage? You heal up. That brings me to my next point: the inventory system. You only have 2 slots for a gun, melee and healing. 2 slots for 3 of these things. You have  to carry something in your hand just so you can have all of these things on you. You also have to hold the lighter to know where to go which doesn't even work properly half of the time. I've never been this mad over a purchase in my entire life. Please don't buy this game. You'll regret it

reviews.review_title: Kind of a poor man's resident evil.
reviews.review_description: I read the reviews for this and thought I'd be in for a great game... its just OK. Alot of people saying its scary? The first "jump scare" with the big guy close to beginning didn't make me flinch I tried to punch the guy out but he just killed me, I guess you have to run away like a sissy girl. Dont get me wrong its abit freaky at times but the campaign is too short and there's not much variety in the way of gameplay its just the same s##t all the way through except for a couple of bosses, certainly not worth the price.

This game is hands down, the best game I’ve played on VR. The graphics are so fun, the gameplay is not impossible like some games, and overall, it’s a wonderfully structured game. Recommend in the highest of ways! Enjoy!
I was fighting the final boss, the enemies weren't effected by the slow motion,and I couldn't pick up the ammo in the middle of the room

reviews.review_title: The full fat survival horror experience
reviews.review_description:
I just finished the first chase sequence a fair few minutes ago and my heart still feels like it could be beating out of my chest. It was exhilerating and well done in how you had to navigate a constrictive space while under pressure. It almost felt like a scene out of a horror movie where a character barely survives a nailbiting sequence by the skin of your teeth. Where a triple A flat horror game would have you mashing buttons in frantic QTE session here it's all depends on you and how fast you can navigate this tight maze.
Of course, chase sequences alone do not make a great horror game and this game has got you covered. While most VR horror experiences are jumpscare based and more 'Paranormal Activity' inspired (and I do love these virtual haunted house attractions) this is more atmosphere/tension based horror which relies a lot on the excellent 'effin with your mind' sound design. You will not be helpless though as in this game you will bear arms making it more of a VR entry to the Survival Horror genre. In fact, it kind of reminded me of Resident Evil 4 in it's vibe and atmosphere even though it's entirely it's own thing. Especially with the comic book like shading and textboxes that appear. These textboxes do not break the immersion but fill in the main characters thoughts and backstory in brief snippets so no overbearing loredumps here.
So to round it off if you made it this far, if you love horror games in VR but want to try something different, more engaging, more console/PC game like full fat experience... This is it chief.
This is what a VR game should be. Great atmosphere, creepy, fun, and engaging. If I were to compare this game to another I’d say this is the resident evil 4 of VR.
So dark and scary ! Awesome game
It’s the most boring experience I’ve ever had and would like a refund

This game could have been great. It is a shame that it is completely ruined by gameplay choices. I'll try to explain because there is really a lot that represent a complete turn off for me. Let's begin with simple stuff : equipment management. It is a pain. You have only 2 slots that you have to manage to put everything you need. And I could have been okay with that. It seems like Half life Alyx style, right? Absolutely not! Because those 2 slots are occupied by your weapons too. You have to choose to bring with you a sandwich to heal or a gun. A gun or an axe. Most of the monsters are unstoppable death machines and bullet sponges that runs towards you to beat you to death, and most of the time they spawn so close to you that you don't actually have the time to kill them before they reach you and hurt you. So you need a source of healing and a lot of fire power in this game to go through. You would say : well okay you have 2 slots, one for a gun one for the food, you are okay. No I'm not. I only briefly mentioned the melee weapons. Those are completely useless in combat (because as I said the enemies are unstoppable death machines careless about an axe hit on the head, so they reach you and beat you no matter what, if you go melee), but you need to bring one with you to break wood obstacles, and to break crates where you can find ammo and food. So the math is simple : to proceed in the game we absolutely need 3 things (a gun, some food and a melee weapon) but we have only 2 slots to manage all of that. Great! At this point you could say : "you can always have your weapons in hand and put something else inside the 2 slots." That would be in perfect Alyx style and would be great. But it doesn't work like that.  If you want to take something from a slot you need to drop your weapon on the floor. And if you are in the middle of a fight you can't even think about dropping a weapon to take your sandwich to heal yourself, because that weapon will always drop inside the floor pixels, or fly miles away from you for no reason. Or if you are lucky enough that it drops on the floor near you, good luck on take it up from there. While 2 or 3 foes are surrounding you, you have to struggle with the "not so precise" items grabbing behavior. And what about if I want to grab a two handed weapon from my back? I have to drop on the floor both my gun and my melee if my hips slots are full. And what about collectibles composed by multiple parts? You have to hold those in your hands bringing them with you looking for other parts... This items management system just doesn't work. But all of these things I mentioned are not the worst part. The worst, obnoxious, degenerate, toxic thing of all : the cursed dolls section. Oh boy. In the middle of those "dreams" sessions you have to run around the map looking for those floating dolls, while a never-ending wave of enemies spawns from everywhere around you. And while you are there, in front of a doll, surrounded by monsters.... You have to drop both your weapons, grab the doll with one hand, take the lighter with the other and burn the doll. Than grab your weapons from the floor, kill everyone around you and go chase the next doll... Absurd... Another thing that I can't understand is monsters respawn. It completely shuts down every form of exploration of the map. Because there is no reason to go searching for ammo or food if when I come back I have to consume what I just found, killing respawned monsters. It has just no sense in my opinion. About the weapons. There is a great variety of weapons. But unfortunately most of those are useless or have a weird concept behind them. Traps and gas cans are fun, so are the throwing knifes or spikes, but this game is not made for single use weapons (due to the awful slot management system we already talked about) so I've never used those. As we already stated melee weapons are useless in combat, but needed for other stuff in the game, and that makes them even more annoying... So what remains are the guns, the shotgun and the harpoon. The guns are cool and handy. Love those. The shotgun ammo management is a mistery to me. For every other weapon you can store more ammo than what the magazine can handle and reload the weapon when needed. Not the shotgun. You can just have those 6 bullets. Why? And than there is the harpoon. Oh boy I loved it. That is really brilliant. You can use it for open hidden paths, to reach dolls from distance, to grab hooks away from you... but you can't use it as a weapon... What a shame... It just stuns the enemy,  it doesn't kill him. Why?... there is no point to stun the enemy if I have to waste time to put the harpoon behind my back and take another weapon to kill him. Anyway... Well this was a hell of a long message but I wrote it because I really think that this game could shine in incredible ways. The graphic is phenomenal, the atmosphere is great, the world is intriguing and well designed, the story is simple but cool. I just can't stand these gameplay choices. And for me the gameplay is the most important thing of all, that's why I had to give the game 1 star. I'm sorry. Of course all of my complaints comes from my personal taste and I can understand if the game works great for others.

reviews.review_title: Hard difficulty is Hard. Really hard.
reviews.review_description:
This game is just excellent. Despite its hardware limitations it has made one of the most beautiful vr games I've seen besides the really recent ones. 

I started off on hard for my first playthrough, I RECOMMEND YOU DO NOT DO THIS. Holy F*** its hard (no s***, lmao). Dont get me wrong, I'm having tons of fun but its reminiscent of trying to play games like doom or the evil within on the hardest difficulty: I.E if you're anything like me you got your cheeks repeatedly clapped and would get caught on certain fights for an extended period of time. I wish there was a dodge option or something so I could avoid getting hit so much. I'm gonna try a bigger play area so I can literally dodge in real life. I recommend this game to ANYONE who considers purchasing it.

You’re gonna need several sessions to beat this game.. Which is great!

PROS:
- Awesome atmosphere of dread
- Great weapon gameplay
- Great inventory management
- Difficulty is great— Leaves me huffing but I can’t wait to go and try to beat the chapter I’m stuck on again.

CONS:
- Enemies can have wonky hit detection. They’ll swing and miss but somehow still hit you.
- The revolver is kinda useless a lot of the time. Melee weapons are the wave in this game.

Can’t get into this game until I find out why everything appears so washed out...  Contrast is markedly bad - blacks are grey.  

Also, you can’t physically move very much in this game without the entire screen flickering to black and then back.  And after each of these blackouts the camera seems to be moved. What’s up with that?

developer_response.body:
Hi Ryan,

Just wanted to respond quickly about the black screen fade issue you mentioned. This functionality occurs to prevent players from room scaling through walls, but it was activating in situations it should not have been. We've fixed this issue internally and expect the fix to make its way into a patch for everyone soon.  Thanks for your patience and feedback!

reviews.review_description: Perfect game, the environment is nice and creepy and the gameplay is fun. There isn’t a lot of pop ups but it still manages to creep you out with the nice soundtrack and monsters coming after you. This game is addicting and I recommend it to anyone looking for a fun game to play with a nice story
I fell and maybe broke my back playing this
Hedjdbfjcixjbd

reviews.review_title: Scared the crap out of me😬
reviews.review_description: I just started Using my new oculus 2 and played this game….I got so scared I tried to run (in real life ) from a monster guy and threw myself on the ground 😬 be careful !! Lol
Love love love. What a game. Thank you so much. This is fabulous and wonderful and I am hooked. It’s so easy for us not-so-smart VR newbies. Excellent directions. Very intuitive design.

I usually am not big into horror games but after I bought my oculus I have been searching for a good game to play and this has exceeded my expectations the combat is perfect for vr and is absolutely a blast to play and I thought the comic book art style would suck but it really ties the game together and would highly recommend this game
I've showed this to 2 of my friends and they loved it like I did
